content.problemStatement:
You are given a permutation p1,p2,‚Ä¶,pNp_1, p_2, \ldots, p_Np1‚Äã,p2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,pN‚Äã. Let's denote a contiguous subsequence pl,pl+1,‚Ä¶,prp_l, p_{l+1}, \ldots, p_rpl‚Äã,pl+1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,pr‚Äã in it by [l,r][l, r][l,r] and call it interesting if max‚Å°(pl,pl+1,‚Ä¶,pr)‚àímin‚Å°(pl,pl+1,‚Ä¶,pr)=r‚àíl\max(p_l, p_{l+1}, \ldots, p_r) - \min(p_l, p_{l+1}, \ldots, p_r) = r - lmax(pl‚Äã,pl+1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,pr‚Äã)‚àímin(pl‚Äã,pl+1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,pr‚Äã)=r‚àíl.

Next, let's say that two interesting subsequences [a,b][a,b][a,b] and [c,d][c,d][c,d] are nested if a‚â§c‚â§d‚â§ba \le c \le d \le ba‚â§c‚â§d‚â§b or c‚â§a‚â§b‚â§dc \le a \le b \le dc‚â§a‚â§b‚â§d, and they are interleaved if max(a,c)‚â§min(b,d)\mathrm{max}(a,c) \le \mathrm{min}(b,d)max(a,c)‚â§min(b,d), but they are not nested. The intersection of two such interleaved subsequences is the subsequence [max(a,c),min(b,d)][\mathrm{max}(a,c), \mathrm{min}(b,d)][max(a,c),min(b,d)].

You need to find the number of ordered pairs of interesting contiguous subsequences such that they are interleaved and their intersection contains at least XXX elements. Since this number may be very large, compute it modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
constraints: 1‚â§pi‚â§N1 \le p_i \le N1‚â§pi‚Äã‚â§N for each valid iii
p1,p2,‚Ä¶,pNp_1, p_2, \ldots, p_Np1‚Äã,p2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,pN‚Äã are pairwise distinct.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers NNN and XXX.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers p1,p2,‚Ä¶,pNp_1, p_2, \ldots, p_Np1‚Äã,p2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,pN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer ‚Äï the number of valid pairs of interesting subsequences modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
